nw_001.title: Natural Harbor
nw_001.body: A perfect bay can become our gateway or just a stop.

nw_002.title: The King's Tax
nw_002.body: The court demands its share before the money has even truly come in.

nw_003.title: Clever Interpreter
nw_003.body: Our interpreter simplifies treaties whenever it suits him.

choices.A: Keep him because he is useful

nw_004.title: Hidden Mine
nw_004.body: A guide speaks of a mine far from the shore and close to trouble.

nw_005.title: Allied Village
nw_005.body: A nearby village offers peace in exchange for tools and respect.

nw_006.title: Hungry Crew
nw_006.body: The sailors want a real feast and not more sad fish.

choices.C: Buy local and invite the port

nw_007.title: Fort or Market
nw_007.body: We do not have enough men or enough money to do everything.

nw_008.title: Maps for Sale
nw_008.body: A cartographer wants to sell our routes to the highest bidder.

nw_009.title: Flag Too Soon
nw_009.body: Planting our colors here would be glorious or deeply reckless.

nw_010.title: Local Harvest
nw_010.body: The harvest is good. Everyone wants their share before sunset.

nw_011.title: Inland Route
nw_011.body: A trail inland promises land and traps in equal measure.

nw_012.title: Wounded Messenger
nw_012.body: The royal messenger falls from his horse with our orders and our prestige.

nw_013.title: Pirate Bay
nw_013.body: A discreet bay appeals to honest merchants and the others too.

nw_014.title: Court School
nw_014.body: The governor wants a school to spread our laws and customs.

nw_015.title: Salt Monopoly
nw_015.body: Local salt is worth a great deal and is already attracting greed.

nw_016.title: Useless Noble
nw_016.body: A noble demands an escort to visit an empty beach in style.

nw_017.title: Missing Guide
nw_017.body: Our best guide vanished with a very visible purse.

nw_018.title: Floating Market
nw_018.body: Vendors arrive by canoe and prefer talking prices over politics.

choices.C: Invite the vendors to the port

nw_019.title: Shared Well
nw_019.body: The camp well also interests the nearby village and its jars.

choices.B: Reserve the water for the camp

nw_020.title: Thieving Sailor
nw_020.body: A discreet sailor has pockets heavier than his pay.

nw_021.title: Temple by the Shore
nw_021.body: A small temple watches over the port and the village's eyes.

nw_022.title: Useful Forest
nw_022.body: Local timber can feed our worksites or upset everyone.

nw_023.title: River Toll
nw_023.body: A local chief demands a toll to pass along the river.

nw_024.title: Rumor of Revolt
nw_024.body: Whispers run through the port streets faster than our boots.

nw_025.title: Empty Royal Treasury
nw_025.body: The fort treasury makes a very convincing empty sound.

choices.C: Ask the king for more time

nw_026.title: Secret Cove
nw_026.body: A hidden cove would be perfect for a depot or an escape.

nw_027.title: Local Festival
nw_027.body: The village is preparing a festival. We are invited or watched, maybe both.

nw_028.title: Ambitious Scout
nw_028.body: A scout wants more men to explore far away and return rich.

choices.B: Give him only a few men

nw_029.title: Foreign Merchant
nw_029.body: A merchant from elsewhere offers a discreet and profitable pact.

nw_030.title: Local Lighthouse
nw_030.body: A modest lighthouse already guides the bay better than our maps.

nw_031.title: Wandering Livestock
nw_031.body: Animals wander near our fields and close to the sailors' desires.

nw_032.title: Tampered Map
nw_032.body: A new map enlarges our lands without moving a single stone.

choices.C: Keep the fake version for ourselves

nw_033.title: Fishing Closed
nw_033.body: Local fishermen ask for two days without boats in their area.

nw_034.title: Slowed Port
nw_034.body: General fatigue is slowing down the port. Nothing serious, but everything is late.

nw_035.title: Chief's House
nw_035.body: The local chief accepts a visit, but not a lesson.

nw_036.title: Port Orphan
nw_036.body: A clever child knows every dock and every shortcut.

choices.C: Put him in service of the fort

nw_037.title: Bell With No Master
nw_037.body: An old bell lies in the square and everyone claims it.

nw_038.title: Buried Treasure
nw_038.body: Workers swear they saw gold under an old stone.

nw_039.title: Hill Route
nw_039.body: A road to the hills opens trade and shuts down sleep.

nw_040.title: Smuggled Rum
nw_040.body: A barrel moves through the night faster than a royal document.

choices.C: Let it pass for a favor

nw_041.title: Muddy Canal
nw_041.body: Digging a canal would save time and drain the treasury.

nw_042.title: Indiscreet Parrot
nw_042.body: The port parrot repeats our secrets better than our scribes do.

nw_043.title: Perfect Tide
nw_043.body: The perfect tide can serve a landing or a spice convoy.

nw_044.title: Local Craftsman
nw_044.body: A craftsman makes better goods than our workshops and faster than our complaints.

nw_045.title: Exposed Farm
nw_045.body: A farm near the fort attracts trade as much as thieves.

nw_046.title: Two Chiefs
nw_046.body: Two neighboring chiefs ask for our support against their common rival.

nw_047.title: Broken Coaster
nw_047.body: A small port boat sinks slowly and very publicly.

nw_048.title: Rainy Season
nw_048.body: Rain can ruin the roads or wash away our mistakes, depending on its mood.

choices.C: Carry on as if nothing is wrong

nw_049.title: Colored Fabric
nw_049.body: A local fabric turns heads and profit ideas.

nw_050.title: Broken Beacon
nw_050.body: The cape beacon has fallen. The sea loves that kind of detail.

nw_051.title: Dock Repairs
nw_051.body: The dock is cracking. It may hold a little longer or not at all.

choices.C: Close the dock for a while

nw_052.title: Talkative Spy
nw_052.body: A man speaks our language far too easily to be just a merchant.

choices.C: Let him talk so we can listen

nw_053.title: Pier Tax
nw_053.body: Private piers spring up fast when gold moves even faster.

nw_054.title: Local Banner
nw_054.body: The village wants its banner to fly beside ours.

nw_055.title: Salty Spring
nw_055.body: A spring gives strange water, but it is useful for preserving goods.

nw_056.title: Useful Deserter
nw_056.body: A man from a rival kingdom wants to sell us his plans.

nw_057.title: Shared Barn
nw_057.body: The village proposes a shared barn to get through the bad season.

choices.B: Let the village handle it alone
choices.C: Take the barn for the fort

nw_058.title: Fast Boats
nw_058.body: Local canoes move better than our heavy service boats.

nw_059.title: Black Powder
nw_059.body: Powder stocks are dropping while the reports rise in panic.

choices.C: Sell part of it to a neighbor

nw_060.title: Market Square
nw_060.body: The central square can become a trading area or a control zone.

nw_061.title: Tired Miners
nw_061.body: The miners want a bonus before going even deeper.

nw_062.title: Letter From the King
nw_062.body: The king asks for a strong gesture to mark our presence here.

nw_063.title: Map Merchant
nw_063.body: A man sells maps of places we have not seen.

choices.C: Take him on as a scout

nw_064.title: Place of Rest
nw_064.body: The workers ask for a proper place to honor those lost on the worksite.

nw_065.title: Rare Herbs
nw_065.body: Local herbs heal well and sell even better.

nw_066.title: Rival Pier
nw_066.body: A rival merchant builds his pier faster than our decisions.

nw_067.title: Blurry Border
nw_067.body: The northern border mostly depends on who speaks the loudest.

choices.B: Invite the chiefs to draw it

nw_068.title: Grain Reserve
nw_068.body: Our grain stocks can secure the winter or bring great profit.

nw_069.title: Nearby Islet
nw_069.body: A small islet controls the bay. It is pretty and highly strategic.

nw_070.title: Creative Accountant
nw_070.body: The accountant always finds the right number for the wrong problem.

choices.A: Keep him because he is useful

nw_071.title: Hunting Forbidden
nw_071.body: The village forbids hunting in a game-rich area.

nw_072.title: Noisy Tavern
nw_072.body: The port tavern earns well but drowns discipline.

nw_073.title: Shared Field
nw_073.body: Empty land near the fort can feed our men or theirs.

nw_074.title: Useful Castaways
nw_074.body: Castaways arrive with tools and questionable habits.

nw_075.title: Court of Justice
nw_075.body: The port wants a real place to settle disputes before knives come out.

nw_076.title: Slow Convoy
nw_076.body: A metal convoy moves so slowly that it draws the attention of every thief.

nw_077.title: Fort Gates
nw_077.body: The fort gates are handsome, heavy, and open far too often.

nw_078.title: Smuggled Salt
nw_078.body: Salt leaves the warehouses faster than water leaks from barrels.

nw_079.title: Proud Guide
nw_079.body: Our guide refuses to walk unless his worth is recognized.

nw_080.title: Village in Transition
nw_080.body: The chief of the neighboring village is gone. Everyone is waiting for our move.

choices.C: Take the area before the mourning ends

nw_081.title: Port Office
nw_081.body: The port office is drowning in paperwork and not in efficiency.

choices.C: Sell the positions to the highest bidder

nw_082.title: Temple Treasure
nw_082.body: The temple keeps an old chest that no one opens without trembling.

choices.C: Seize it in the king's name

nw_083.title: Rival Company
nw_083.body: Another European company offers a deal that smells like a polite trap.

choices.C: Keep them away from the port

nw_084.title: Blocked Mill
nw_084.body: The port mill is blocked by a stone and a dispute.

nw_085.title: Local Flag
nw_085.body: A village wants to raise its flag during our official parade.

choices.C: Accept in exchange for public tribute

nw_086.title: Cabin Boy's Sling
nw_086.body: The cabin boy shoots accurately and already wants to lead a small group of scouts.

choices.C: Put him in charge of supplies

nw_087.title: Useful Reef
nw_087.body: A reef protects the bay. It can also ruin a landing.

nw_088.title: Spice House
nw_088.body: A port house wants our protection against a local gang.

nw_089.title: Indigo Field
nw_089.body: Local indigo is valuable, but it takes land that feeds people better.

nw_090.title: Old Tower
nw_090.body: An old tower on the shore can serve as an outpost or fall onto our boots.

choices.B: Dismantle it and sell the stone

nw_091.title: Night Market
nw_091.body: The night market sells everything, even what we lost yesterday.

nw_092.title: Sacred Clearing
nw_092.body: A sacred clearing blocks our best wagon route.

nw_093.title: Missing Chest
nw_093.body: A chest disappeared between the dock, the fort, and some very innocent looks.

nw_094.title: Fertile Lands
nw_094.body: A fertile plain can feed the port or enrich three notable men.

nw_095.title: Weapons Workshop
nw_095.body: A blacksmith wants to open a workshop for our blades and our neighbors'.

nw_096.title: Wounded Chief
nw_096.body: An allied chief is wounded. His family is already looking at us sideways.

nw_097.title: Gold Tax
nw_097.body: Gold moves more smoothly when everyone forgets the official tax.

choices.C: Turn a blind eye for a payment

nw_098.title: Fort Entrance
nw_098.body: The fort is growing. We must choose who enters and who waits outside.

nw_099.title: Watchtower
nw_099.body: A watchtower lets you see far away, but it is expensive to build and maintain.

nw_100.title: Masterless Bay
nw_100.body: The finest bay on the coast still belongs to no one.

choices.A: Claim it in the king's name
choices.C: Sign a pact with the villages

nw_101.title: Boats Too Slow
nw_101.body: Our boats move far too slowly, and the whole port can see it.

nw_102.title: Giant Fish
nw_102.body: A huge fish blocks the harbor entrance as if it paid rent.

nw_103.title: Sleepy Scribe
nw_103.body: The dock scribe stamps everything except the right papers.

nw_104.title: Market in the Rain
nw_104.body: The market goes on despite the rain, but the stalls are almost underwater.

choices.B: Move the market to the fort

nw_105.title: Shore Drum
nw_105.body: A local drum calls the village faster than our bell.

nw_106.title: Leaky Purse
nw_106.body: The person in charge of the cash box is losing too many coins, and no one knows exactly where they are disappearing.

choices.A: Check the cash box with him, coin by coin
choices.B: Give him an assistant to check everything

nw_107.title: Wonder Net
nw_107.body: A fisherman brings in more fish than our salt reserves.

nw_108.title: Crowded Dock
nw_108.body: The dock is full of crates, goats, and excuses.

nw_109.title: Port Oracle
nw_109.body: An old sailor predicts the tide and sometimes the prices.

nw_110.title: Camp Pigs
nw_110.body: The camp pigs eat better than some soldiers.

nw_111.title: Stubborn River
nw_111.body: The river has changed course, and that is making travel and trade more difficult.

nw_112.title: Fragrant Wood
nw_112.body: A rare wood smells so good even the treasury notices.

nw_113.title: Talkative Guard
nw_113.body: A guard shares our plans between two gulps.

choices.A: Transfer him to the north wall

nw_114.title: Broken Wagon
nw_114.body: The port's only large wagon is held together by goodwill.

choices.C: Carry things by hand a while longer

nw_115.title: New Banner
nw_115.body: A clean banner would give pride to a very tired fort.

nw_116.title: Lost Spices
nw_116.body: A crate of spices smells strong but remains nowhere to be found.

nw_117.title: Rope Bridge
nw_117.body: A rope bridge shortens the road and lengthens the prayers.

nw_118.title: Local Honey
nw_118.body: Local honey sticks to fingers and profit ideas alike.

nw_119.title: Blurred Trail
nw_119.body: The path to the hills mostly exists in stories.

nw_120.title: Royal Yard Sale
nw_120.body: Used items from the fort could bring in more than they are worth.

nw_121.title: Jealous Sailors
nw_121.body: The king's sailors envy the faster local canoes.

nw_122.title: Nearby Orchard
nw_122.body: A nearby orchard offers fruit and ideas for settlement.

nw_123.title: Painted Boat
nw_123.body: A local boat painted in bright colors draws the whole dock.

nw_124.title: Gourmet Chief
nw_124.body: The neighboring chief loves our bread but hates our long speeches.

choices.B: Sell him the bread at a high price

nw_125.title: Empty Warehouse
nw_125.body: Our warehouse is almost empty, and that is starting to create a real supply problem.

nw_126.title: Rare Rope
nw_126.body: Good rope is scarce, and bad rope breaks at the perfect moment.

choices.A: Buy it at a high price

nw_127.title: Sacred Crab
nw_127.body: Crabs are invading the depot and no one dares touch them.

nw_128.title: Charming Merchant
nw_128.body: A merchant seems so friendly that it only makes him more suspicious.

nw_129.title: Fort Roof
nw_129.body: The fort roof leaks with a true sense of drama.

nw_130.title: Fishing Agreement
nw_130.body: Two villages want the same fishing zone and our opinion.

choices.B: Sell the zone to the highest bidder

nw_131.title: Suspicious Smoke
nw_131.body: Strange smoke rises from the hills, and not from our kitchen.

nw_132.title: Boat Without Oars
nw_132.body: A new boat arrives without oars and with a great deal of confidence.

nw_133.title: Dockside Inn
nw_133.body: An inn opens and promises rest, rumors, and weak bills.

nw_134.title: Wet Map
nw_134.body: Our best map has been damaged by water.

nw_135.title: Camp Tomatoes
nw_135.body: The camp garden finally succeeds at something round.

nw_136.title: The Noble's Stroll
nw_136.body: A noble wants to visit the marshes in white boots.

nw_137.title: Thieving Seagulls
nw_137.body: The dock seagulls steal more bread than the children do.

choices.B: Sell bread at a higher price

nw_138.title: Red Earth
nw_138.body: Red soil promises good bricks and very dirty boots.

choices.C: Reserve the area for the fort

nw_139.title: Taste of Salt
nw_139.body: The local salt is excellent, and our cooks are growing arrogant.

choices.B: Keep the best for the fort

nw_140.title: Quiet Canoe
nw_140.body: A small canoe arrives every night and never seems in a hurry.

nw_141.title: Deep Well
nw_141.body: The new well gives clear water and plenty of opinions.

nw_142.title: Rare Flour
nw_142.body: Flour is running out, and the bakers are waiting for help that never comes.

nw_143.title: Cracked Dike
nw_143.body: A dike is cracking, and the sea already has an opinion.

nw_144.title: Found Jewel
nw_144.body: A jewel found on the beach makes every eye shine.

choices.B: Offer it to the neighboring chief

nw_145.title: Southern Vineyard
nw_145.body: A local vineyard yields light wine and very heavy debates.

choices.B: Invite the chiefs to taste it
choices.C: Reserve the barrels for the fort

nw_146.title: Useful Sand
nw_146.body: The bay sand suits construction better than our moods do.

nw_147.title: Proud Carpenter
nw_147.body: The best carpenter wants more money and more praise.

nw_148.title: Port Meal
nw_148.body: The shared port meal can calm complaints or feed them.

nw_149.title: Local Pie
nw_149.body: A local pie is popular as long as we can give it a royal name.

choices.B: Let the village make a living from it

nw_150.title: Construction Mud
nw_150.body: The worksite moves slower than the mud on our boots.

nw_151.title: Leaning Pier
nw_151.body: The new dock is already leaning. If it is not reinforced, it will quickly become dangerous.

nw_152.title: Dock Dog
nw_152.body: A local dog guards crates better than two awake men.

choices.C: Feed it for the whole port

nw_153.title: Missing Rowers
nw_153.body: Half the rowers have found a better excuse than the sea.

nw_154.title: Quiet Pepper
nw_154.body: Pepper moves beneath the dock as if it had legs.

nw_155.title: Stone Wall
nw_155.body: A stone wall would reassure the camp and empty the treasury.

nw_156.title: Island Fair
nw_156.body: Island merchants want a fair full of noise and smells.

nw_157.title: Brutal Sun
nw_157.body: The heat drains strength faster than water flasks.

nw_158.title: Missing Nail
nw_158.body: Dock work stops because of one nail no one can find.

nw_159.title: Calm Basin
nw_159.body: A natural basin could shelter small ships without drama.

nw_160.title: Missing Boots
nw_160.body: Boots keep disappearing from the camp, swallowed by the mud or lost in the chaos.

choices.C: Let the men manage on their own

nw_161.title: Engraved Stone
nw_161.body: An engraved stone fascinates the port more than our morning orders.

nw_162.title: New Smokehouse
nw_162.body: A smokehouse would preserve fish longer than our promises.

nw_163.title: Hasty Neighbor
nw_163.body: A neighbor wants a quick treaty before the coffee is even served.

nw_164.title: Broken Mast
nw_164.body: A ship loses its mast in the harbor with a very convincing crash.

choices.C: Reserve the dock for our own

nw_165.title: Abundant Rice
nw_165.body: Local rice grows so well that even the mice are getting fat.

nw_166.title: Office Too Clean
nw_166.body: The new office chief puts everything away, including what should be easy to find quickly.

choices.C: Assign a local assistant to him

nw_167.title: Short Tide
nw_167.body: The tide drops quickly, and our large ships are already complaining.

nw_168.title: Fort Straw
nw_168.body: The fort straw attracts fire and mice with equal talent.

nw_169.title: Laughing Village
nw_169.body: The village laughs at our helmets that are too hot and too shiny.

nw_170.title: Salt Road
nw_170.body: A salt road could enrich the bay or drain our guards.

nw_171.title: Talkative Shoemaker
nw_171.body: The shoemaker knows every secret of the port and talks far too much.

nw_172.title: Broken Baskets
nw_172.body: The market baskets make pineapples move faster than the vendor.

nw_173.title: Days of Rest
nw_173.body: The workers ask for one day of rest before getting back to work.

choices.B: Pay a bonus to keep going

nw_174.title: New Bugle
nw_174.body: A new bugle would make more noise than strategy.

choices.C: Sell the idea to the noble

nw_175.title: Toolbox
nw_175.body: A full toolbox finally arrives without the key to open it.

nw_176.title: Sugar Cane
nw_176.body: Sugar cane grows well and makes the ledgers very greedy.

nw_177.title: Official Hammock
nw_177.body: An officer wants hammocks to improve sleep strategy.

nw_178.title: Golden Paddle
nw_178.body: A decorated paddle is being passed around the camp, but it is useless.

choices.A: Give it to the best rower

nw_179.title: Flooded Field
nw_179.body: A field near the port is drinking more water than expected.

nw_180.title: Singing Ship
nw_180.body: A crew sings so loudly that the whole port knows our schedule.

choices.B: Have them sing at the market

nw_181.title: Rigged Weights
nw_181.body: Some market weights are lighter than the truth.

nw_182.title: Small Lighthouse
nw_182.body: A small private lighthouse guides better than our grand speech.

nw_183.title: Dockside Coffee
nw_183.body: A coffee corner opens, and decisions are made there before they reach the office.

nw_184.title: Stolen Anchor
nw_184.body: An anchor disappears, and suddenly everyone is watching the sea.

nw_185.title: Overloaded Hold
nw_185.body: The hold of the royal ship is so full that organization is starting to break down.

choices.B: Sell part of it on site
choices.C: Reserve the cargo for the king

nw_186.title: Smart Bamboo
nw_186.body: Local bamboo is useful for everything and knows it very well.

choices.C: Reserve the bamboo for the fort

nw_187.title: Polite Rivals
nw_187.body: Two rivals want our support with smiles that are too perfect.

nw_188.title: Damp Ledgers
nw_188.body: The port records are taking on water and losing their memory.

nw_189.title: Shaded Square
nw_189.body: A shaded square could draw sales and calm tempers.

nw_190.title: Quiet Pearls
nw_190.body: Pearls are moving through pockets more than through the treasury.

choices.A: Buy the lot at the port

nw_191.title: Royal Canoe
nw_191.body: A canoe offered to the king would make a good impression and hurt the budget.

nw_192.title: Dock Paving Stones
nw_192.body: The dirt dock swallows wheels with great appetite.

nw_193.title: Hot Granary
nw_193.body: The granary is getting too hot, and the grain stock is starting to spoil.

choices.C: Lend part of it to the village

nw_194.title: Trading Street
nw_194.body: A street in the port wants to get rich before it even gets clean.

nw_195.title: The King's Mark
nw_195.body: The king wants his symbol everywhere, even on turnip crates.

nw_196.title: Market Goat
nw_196.body: A local goat eats its way through the stalls with real taste.

nw_197.title: Driftwood
nw_197.body: The shore brings in driftwood without asking for wages.

nw_198.title: Torn Sail
nw_198.body: A large sail tears before the simplest crossing.

nw_199.title: Hasty Merchants
nw_199.body: The merchants want to open before dawn and close before our inspections.

nw_200.title: Port Hill
nw_200.body: A nearby hill overlooks the whole port and already dreams of a tower.

nw_201.title: Rusty Bell
nw_201.body: The port bell rings badly, and no one knows whether it is a real alarm.

choices.C: Replace the alarm with a whistle

nw_202.title: Overripe Bananas
nw_202.body: Bananas are threatening the dock and the noses of passersby.

nw_203.title: Laughing Fog
nw_203.body: A thick fog hides the port and makes orders hard to carry out.

nw_204.title: Unnamed Crate
nw_204.body: A crate arrives with no mark, no papers, and plenty of nerve.

nw_205.title: Single File
nw_205.body: A long line forms at the dock, and everyone swears they are the priority.

choices.C: Let the port sort it out

nw_206.title: Varnished Boat
nw_206.body: A local boat shines so brightly that we see its reflection before its sails.

nw_207.title: Sneaky Wave
nw_207.body: A low wave wets the cargo whenever the port gets proud.

nw_208.title: Twisted Sign
nw_208.body: The dock sign points three ways and helps none of them.

nw_209.title: Leaky Fish Trap
nw_209.body: The fish traps lose more catch than the fishermen do.

nw_210.title: Port Whistle
nw_210.body: The new whistle does a poor job of alerting the guards and mostly disturbs the residents.

nw_211.title: Flooded Garden
nw_211.body: The camp garden looks like green soup after the rain.

nw_212.title: Headwind
nw_212.body: The wind pushes our sails the wrong way with real conviction.

nw_213.title: Night Rain
nw_213.body: The night rain turns the camp into a chorus of drops.

nw_214.title: Giant Squash
nw_214.body: Giant squash invades the garden like big orange lords.

nw_215.title: Sun on Tar
nw_215.body: The dock tar melts a little, and the boots keep the memory.

nw_216.title: Land Too Dry
nw_216.body: The ground is cracking, and even the seeds hesitate.

nw_217.title: Greedy Field
nw_217.body: A field devours our seed bags faster than it delivers promises.

nw_218.title: Nest on the Tower
nw_218.body: Birds have chosen the tower as their official palace.

nw_219.title: Tall Grass
nw_219.body: The grass near the fort hides more things than our records do.

nw_220.title: Harvest Too Early
nw_220.body: Workers want to harvest quickly before the weather turns.

nw_221.title: Torn Tent
nw_221.body: The large tent lets wind through from every side. It protects the camp poorly.

nw_222.title: Angry Cauldron
nw_222.body: The large cauldron burns the bottom and the cook's mood.

nw_223.title: Barefoot Soldiers
nw_223.body: Some boots have vanished, and the soldiers stare at the ground in blame.

nw_224.title: Distracted Watchman
nw_224.body: The watchman watches the sea but forgets the dock.

nw_225.title: Broken Bench
nw_225.body: The office bench gives way under the heaviest visitors.

nw_226.title: Forgotten Canteen
nw_226.body: The workers forget their canteens and end up grumbling very dryly.

nw_227.title: Expensive Lanterns
nw_227.body: Lanterns cost more than a merchant's smile.

nw_228.title: Local Blanket
nw_228.body: A local blanket keeps people warm and costs less than our complaints.

nw_229.title: Poorly Stored Paddles
nw_229.body: The depot paddles disappear into their own mess.

nw_230.title: Line at the Well
nw_230.body: The line at the well grows longer every morning. Access to water is becoming too slow.

nw_231.title: Village Song
nw_231.body: A local song mocks the fort, and everyone knows the chorus.

nw_232.title: Awkward Gift
nw_232.body: A neighboring chief offers a large drum that takes up more space than meaning.

nw_233.title: Evening Dance
nw_233.body: The village invites the camp to a dance that may be worth more than a treaty.

nw_234.title: Late Chief
nw_234.body: The neighboring chief always arrives late but always smiling.

nw_235.title: Map Seller
nw_235.body: A local seller promises accurate maps and a smile that costs money.

nw_236.title: Shared Fishing
nw_236.body: The village proposes a shared fishing day to calm two disputes.

choices.C: Keep our men in the fort

nw_237.title: Questionable Translation
nw_237.body: Our interpreter sometimes turns a greeting into a debate.

nw_238.title: Official Hat
nw_238.body: The neighboring chiefs laugh at our large ceremonial hat.

nw_239.title: Shared Path
nw_239.body: The camp and the village use the same path and the same dust.

nw_240.title: Gift Rumor
nw_240.body: A rumor announces a royal gift that does not yet exist.

nw_241.title: Monkey in the Granary
nw_241.body: A monkey steals grain with the precision of a tax collector.

nw_242.title: Dock Ducks
nw_242.body: Some ducks occupy the dock as if they were paying the guard.

nw_243.title: Famous Donkey
nw_243.body: A local donkey carries loads so well that everyone talks about it.

nw_244.title: Talkative Parrot
nw_244.body: A parrot repeats orders before they become useful.

choices.B: Keep it away from the office

nw_245.title: Persistent Turtle
nw_245.body: A turtle crosses the yard every day and gets in the way.

nw_246.title: Climbing Goat
nw_246.body: A goat climbs onto the crates and always chooses the most expensive ones.

nw_247.title: Depot Cat
nw_247.body: A depot cat hunts mice well but sleeps through inventory.

nw_248.title: Thieving Heron
nw_248.body: A heron steals small fish from the market with insulting calm.

nw_249.title: Champion Mosquitoes
nw_249.body: The swamp mosquitoes win every evening battle.

nw_250.title: Nervous Horse
nw_250.body: The messenger's horse startles even at an empty bucket.

nw_251.title: Slippery Shore
nw_251.body: The new shore slips more than it serves.

nw_252.title: Dock Stairs
nw_252.body: The dock stairs lose a step with every serious rain.

nw_253.title: Observation Tower
nw_253.body: A small tower would offer a fine view and long expenses.

nw_254.title: Wet Rope
nw_254.body: Wet ropes weigh like broken promises.

nw_255.title: Small Lock
nw_255.body: A small lock could calm the basin waters.

nw_256.title: Loose Stakes
nw_256.body: Some stakes shift in the mud as if they wanted to leave.

nw_257.title: Rolling Barrel
nw_257.body: A barrel rolls down the dock on its own, and no one thanked it.

nw_258.title: Makeshift Crane
nw_258.body: A patched-together crane lifts crates and our worries.

nw_259.title: Plank Path
nw_259.body: A plank path would prevent many ridiculous falls.

choices.B: Make the merchants pay for it

nw_260.title: Depot Key
nw_260.body: The depot key changes pockets faster than the accounts do.

choices.C: Leave it open during the day

nw_261.title: Local Soap
nw_261.body: A local soap smells good and cleans better than our speeches.

nw_262.title: Shiny Fabric
nw_262.body: A shiny fabric attracts eyes and maybe coins.

nw_263.title: Polished Shells
nw_263.body: Polished shells please visitors more than they please our soldiers.

nw_264.title: Miracle Tool
nw_264.body: A craftsman praises a tool that cuts everything except the price.

nw_265.title: Smoky Flavor
nw_265.body: A smoked wood gives meats an almost outrageous success.

nw_266.title: Light Stone
nw_266.body: A pale stone lightens the walls and the arms.

nw_267.title: Rare Feather
nw_267.body: A rare feather pleases the scribes so much that they forget the ink.

nw_268.title: Sturdy Pottery
nw_268.body: At last, a local pottery survives hurried hands.

nw_269.title: Well-Made Buckets
nw_269.body: Local buckets hold water better than our old ones.

nw_270.title: Local Ink
nw_270.body: A local ink dries fast and stains with talent.

nw_271.title: Small Tax
nw_271.body: A small tax on the dock would bring in money without too much grumbling.

nw_272.title: Market Dispute
nw_272.body: Two vendors are fighting over the best shaded spot in the market.

nw_273.title: Crumpled Contract
nw_273.body: A contract arrives crumpled, wet, and highly contested.

nw_274.title: Overzealous Guard
nw_274.body: A guard checks everything, even empty baskets and tempers.

nw_275.title: Night Patrol Missing
nw_275.body: The night patrol sometimes forgets itself all on its own.

nw_276.title: King's Scale
nw_276.body: The grand official scale is impressive but blocks half the market.

nw_277.title: Waiting Line
nw_277.body: The office line almost reaches the kitchen.

nw_278.title: Half-Open Door
nw_278.body: The depot door often stays half open out of habit or laziness.

choices.C: Leave it that way during the day

nw_279.title: Salt Office
nw_279.body: The salt passes through too many hands before reaching the accounts.

choices.C: Hand it over to the village

nw_280.title: Right of Passage
nw_280.body: The wagons want to pass quickly and pay slowly.

nw_281.title: Shared Boat
nw_281.body: Two merchants want the same boat on the same morning.

nw_282.title: Tired Mule
nw_282.body: The depot mule now moves only in response to praise.

nw_283.title: Wobbly Wheel
nw_283.body: A wobbly wheel gives the cart the style of a sad dancer.

nw_284.title: Load Too Heavy
nw_284.body: A cargo that is too heavy threatens to break more than tempers.

nw_285.title: Fragile Cargo
nw_285.body: Fine pottery arrives with the nervousness of very expensive glass.

nw_286.title: Departure Before Dawn
nw_286.body: A convoy wants to leave before dawn to catch the cool air.

nw_287.title: Empty Ship
nw_287.body: A ship is leaving almost empty, and that wounds our pride.

nw_288.title: Shortcut Path
nw_288.body: A short path crosses land that no one really owns.

nw_289.title: New Loading Frame
nw_289.body: A loading frame would make maneuvers less ridiculous.

choices.B: Make the merchants pay for it

nw_290.title: Mobile Workforce
nw_290.body: Workers keep moving from one worksite to another at the slightest setback.

nw_291.title: Flag Festival
nw_291.body: The new flag deserves a celebration, but finances are already at their lowest.

nw_292.title: Speech Too Long
nw_292.body: The commander's speech lasts longer than a slow tide.

nw_293.title: Local Medal
nw_293.body: The village offers a woven medal for the most useful people.

nw_294.title: Chief Cook's Apron
nw_294.body: The head cook wants a very luxurious embroidered apron.

nw_295.title: Embroidered Banner
nw_295.body: A banner embroidered by the village would look fine at the fort.

choices.B: Negotiate it in exchange for help

nw_296.title: Bench of Honor
nw_296.body: The square wants a bench of honor for important visitors.

nw_297.title: Rainy Parade
nw_297.body: Rain threatens the small parade planned on the dock.

nw_298.title: Leaf Crown
nw_298.body: A leaf crown is becoming the fashion among the port chiefs.

nw_299.title: King's Square
nw_299.body: They propose renaming the market square after the king.

nw_300.title: Port Day
nw_300.body: A tax-free day would fill the dock with people and noise.

nw_301.title: Royal Wheelbarrow
nw_301.body: The old dock wheelbarrow squeaks louder than the commander.

nw_302.title: Rain on the Ledgers
nw_302.body: The office papers drink the rain with far too much enthusiasm.

nw_303.title: Quiet Cove
nw_303.body: A calm cove could serve better than it sleeps.

nw_304.title: Beans Too Proud
nw_304.body: The camp’s beans grow so well that the local market is starting to suffer from it.

nw_305.title: Spare Rope
nw_305.body: The depot's last good rope has already lived too long.

nw_306.title: Pushy Merchant
nw_306.body: A merchant wants to sign before he has even sat down.

nw_307.title: Black Sand
nw_307.body: A dark sand draws attention as much as profit ideas.

nw_308.title: Office Window
nw_308.body: The office lacks air, and so do the accounts.

nw_309.title: Leaning Tree
nw_309.body: A tree by the dock leans as if it were listening to everything.

nw_310.title: Empty Basins
nw_310.body: The camp basins disappeared faster than the excuses.

nw_311.title: Local Sandals
nw_311.body: Local sandals handle the mud better than our proud boots.

nw_312.title: Useful Rock
nw_312.body: A large rock on the shore seems ready to work for us.

nw_313.title: Night Too Bright
nw_313.body: The camp fires shine so brightly that even the crabs can see us coming.

nw_314.title: Accounts Table
nw_314.body: The office table wobbles and makes work difficult.

nw_315.title: Night Fishing
nw_315.body: Some fishermen want to go out after sunset.

nw_316.title: Shiny Stall
nw_316.body: A new stall attracts the whole market and a great deal of jealousy.

nw_317.title: Camp Rooster
nw_317.body: The rooster wakes everyone except the most useful people.

nw_318.title: Reed Mats
nw_318.body: Reed mats make floors less sad and less slippery.

nw_319.title: Leaky Boat
nw_319.body: A boat takes on water with exemplary regularity.

nw_320.title: Official Bouquet
nw_320.body: The fort hall smells more of dust than of the coming visit.

nw_321.title: Crabs on the Path
nw_321.body: The dock path is crawling with very uncooperative crabs.

nw_322.title: Warm Stone
nw_322.body: A flat stone keeps heat better than our hearths do.

nw_323.title: Market Gate
nw_323.body: The market gate jams the crates and the tempers.

nw_324.title: Quiet Spring
nw_324.body: A small spring runs behind the grass without showing off.

nw_325.title: Painted Canoe
nw_325.body: A colorful canoe gets more people talking than a treaty.

nw_326.title: Salt Wagon
nw_326.body: The salt wagon threatens to end up as a white pile on the road.

nw_327.title: Workshop Roof
nw_327.body: The workshop roof leaks onto the most expensive tools.

nw_328.title: Sick Sentry
nw_328.body: The sentry coughs more than he watches.

nw_329.title: Fire Too Large
nw_329.body: The big evening fire impresses but devours wood.

nw_330.title: Lined-Up Jars
nw_330.body: A row of jars makes people want to fill ten more.

nw_331.title: Colored Glass
nw_331.body: Pieces of shiny glass turn every head.

nw_332.title: Parade Mast
nw_332.body: A tall mast would give the port a very official air.

nw_333.title: Wooden Office
nw_333.body: The dock lacks a truly dry place to sign things without drama.

nw_334.title: Medicinal Herbs
nw_334.body: A healer praises herbs that smell strong and reassure well.

nw_335.title: Savory Pie
nw_335.body: A port pie would almost sell itself.

nw_336.title: Light Rain
nw_336.body: A light rain troubles everything a little without really stopping anything.

nw_337.title: Red Rope
nw_337.body: A rope dyed red attracts eyes more than arms.

nw_338.title: Deep Anchorage
nw_338.body: A deep area can welcome larger ships than we are used to.

nw_339.title: Poorly Tied Sacks
nw_339.body: The storage bags are badly tied and are losing their contents.

nw_340.title: Muddy Alley
nw_340.body: One path in the camp is so muddy that people easily lose their sandals there.

nw_341.title: Star Fruit
nw_341.body: A local fruit is so loved that people talk about it more than the king.

nw_342.title: Wet Drum
nw_342.body: The port drum sounds bad. The signals are becoming hard to understand.

nw_343.title: Crooked Table
nw_343.body: A sorting table tilts so much that all the bags fall to the same side.

nw_344.title: Small Mill
nw_344.body: A small mill could spare many arms.

nw_345.title: Dock Duty
nw_345.body: A light tax would make just the right amount of grumbling.

nw_346.title: Public Hammocks
nw_346.body: Hammocks at the port would keep tired men from falling down so often.

nw_347.title: Covered Market
nw_347.body: The market has dreamed of a roof through many damp moons.

nw_348.title: Missing Ladder
nw_348.body: A ladder is always missing when height becomes urgent.

nw_349.title: Smoked Honey
nw_349.body: A smoked honey surprises everyone and becomes popular very quickly.

nw_350.title: Lighthouse Path
nw_350.body: The lighthouse path breaks ankles and the will to climb.

nw_351.title: Work Gloves
nw_351.body: The workers’ hands are so damaged that something must be done.

nw_352.title: Golden Ear
nw_352.body: A very strong ear of grain gives the granary ideas of grandeur.

nw_353.title: The Chief's Dog
nw_353.body: The neighboring chief's dog loves our food and our hallways.

choices.B: Keep it away from the dock

nw_354.title: Wood Reserve
nw_354.body: The woodpile is shrinking faster than the cook's complaints.

nw_355.title: Narrow Door
nw_355.body: The depot's small door hates large crates.

nw_356.title: Talkative Seller
nw_356.body: A seller draws such a crowd that you hear him more than the port itself.

nw_357.title: Tool Shed
nw_357.body: The hammers travel too much and come back too little.

choices.B: Use a room in the fort
choices.C: Leave the tools in the depot

nw_358.title: Great Mirror
nw_358.body: An enormous mirror arrives at the dock with more pride than caution.

nw_359.title: Slow Patrol
nw_359.body: The evening patrol is moving far too slowly.

nw_360.title: Steep Bank
nw_360.body: A useful bank is too steep for the camp boots.

nw_361.title: Blue Lantern
nw_361.body: A blue lantern lights better than all our seriousness.

nw_362.title: Port Oven
nw_362.body: The dock oven heats poorly and angers people very well.

nw_363.title: Laundry in the Wind
nw_363.body: The sailors' laundry turns the dock into a forest of sheets.

nw_364.title: New Oar
nw_364.body: A well-made oar makes people want to replace the whole stock.

nw_365.title: Fish Festival
nw_365.body: A fish festival would fill the port with smells and coins.

nw_366.title: Silent Docks
nw_366.body: The dock seems so calm that it almost feels like we missed something.

nw_367.title: Smooth Stone
nw_367.body: A polished stone slips less and costs even less than a fall.

nw_368.title: Rain Gear
nw_368.body: The guard ends up soaked before the middle of the day.

nw_369.title: Goat Trail
nw_369.body: A small goat trail climbs better than our grand road.

nw_370.title: Annex Office
nw_370.body: The main office is already spilling over benches and nerves.

nw_371.title: Dry Nets
nw_371.body: Good dry nets are almost worth more than a fine speech.

nw_372.title: Painted Wall
nw_372.body: A colorful wall attracts the crowd far more than a very straight wall.

choices.B: Paint the king's sign on it

nw_373.title: Round Bread
nw_373.body: A new bread is already spreading faster than port news.

nw_374.title: Fragile Package
nw_374.body: The package almost trembles by itself when you look at it.

nw_375.title: Empty Cage
nw_375.body: One cage stays empty in the depot and serves no purpose.

nw_376.title: Old Canoe
nw_376.body: An old canoe is still holding together, but it seems to survive more by miracle than by strength.

nw_377.title: Nearby Reef
nw_377.body: A nearby reef can help with fishing, but it can also get in the way of boat maneuvers.

nw_378.title: Camp Sheets
nw_378.body: The camp sheets have the color of exhaustion.

choices.C: Reserve the new ones for the wounded

nw_379.title: Royal Seal
nw_379.body: The great seal gives weight to papers and time to the lines.

choices.C: Keep the seal in the chest

nw_380.title: Spice Garden
nw_380.body: The garden behind the fort already smells like money.

nw_381.title: Broken Board
nw_381.body: A broken board always chooses the worst possible moment.

nw_382.title: Fast Scribe
nw_382.body: A scribe copies so quickly that we could easily find him another stack.

nw_383.title: Private Pier
nw_383.body: A merchant has built his small pier without waiting for our mood.

nw_384.title: Sweet Barrel
nw_384.body: A very sweet barrel attracts tongues and ideas alike.

nw_385.title: Crate of Soap
nw_385.body: A crate of soap smells of cleanliness and trade.

nw_386.title: Flat Ground
nw_386.body: A nice flat piece of land makes storage and markets dream.

nw_387.title: Shade of the Fig Tree
nw_387.body: The shade of a large fig tree is already worth three benches.

choices.B: Reserve the shade for the office

nw_388.title: Soaked Guard
nw_388.body: The gate guard looks like an armed sponge.

nw_389.title: Path Marker
nw_389.body: A single marker guides the road when it really needs five.

nw_390.title: Morning Market
nw_390.body: The market wants to open earlier than the office sun.

nw_391.title: Marked Stone
nw_391.body: An engraved stone draws attention that we did not ask for.

nw_392.title: High Wave
nw_392.body: A wave higher than usual hits the dock with confidence.

nw_393.title: Complaint Office
nw_393.body: Complaints pile up like badly stacked sacks.

nw_394.title: Fresh Straw
nw_394.body: Fresh straw would do the animals and the noses some good.

nw_395.title: Neighboring Ship
nw_395.body: A ship from a nearby port is unsure about trading with us.

nw_396.title: Public Arbor
nw_396.body: An arbor would give the square shade and the lines some peace.

nw_397.title: Missing Stake
nw_397.body: One hole in the fence is enough to make the whole dock nervous.

nw_398.title: Local Headdress
nw_398.body: A local headdress is so admired that people already want to wear it at the fort.

nw_399.title: Sacks of Flour
nw_399.body: The flour sacks like neither water nor delays.

nw_400.title: Shore Tower
nw_400.body: A tower by the water would make it possible to see everything, but it would cost a fortune.
